items.project_definition: The RESTful interface built with FastAPI providing endpoints for authentication, educational content management, and student registration.
The quantitative goal of reaching 80% or more test coverage on the business logic layer using pytest-cov.
A validation rule that prevents the removal of an educational offering if students are currently linked to it, triggering a 409 Conflict response.
The architectural requirement that all database interactions must utilize non-blocking I/O via AsyncSession and await keywords.
A non-blocking HTTP client used within pytest fixtures to simulate requests to the application.
The database session factory context used to manage asynchronous transactions and unit-of-work patterns.
A development strategy prioritizing the security layer to establish trust boundaries and role-based access control before domain logic implementation.
The security subsystem comprising token generation, verification, and password hashing logic.
The offloading of transactional mail delivery to a non-blocking task to avoid latency in the registration response.
A FastAPI feature used to execute the Resend SDK call after the HTTP response has been returned to the client.
A custom exception raised when a domain constraint, such as the active enrollment check, is failed.
The mechanism for managing cross-origin resource sharing to allow the frontend to interact with the server.
The four foundational persistent storage mutation primitives applied to course management.
The centralized environment variable management system located in the core directory.

items.contextual_anchor: Phase 3: Resend Email Integration as Service
items.project_definition: An exception raised when a mandatory environment variable, such as the email provider key, is missing.
An educational entity owned by an instructor, containing modules and a pricing attribute.
The sequence management of modules within a learning path, enforced via a numerical column.
The Pydantic input model for initializing a new educational entity and its associated modules.
The Pydantic output model providing the public-facing data of an educational entity, including its modules.
The Pydantic model for modifying existing attributes of an educational offering.
The process of transforming passwords into secure strings using passlib for storage.
The PostgreSQL relational storage system used for all persistence.
The structural definition of the data relations and schema mappings.
The collection of external libraries including FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and Pydantic required for execution.
The structured message body used for welcome communications, currently defined as a hardcoded string.
A many-to-many relationship entity linking a student to a course, tracking progress from 0 to 100.
The Pydantic model for initiating a student's link to a specific educational offering.
The Pydantic model for retrieving the current status and progress of a student's course participation.
The Pydantic model used to modify the completion percentage and date of a student's course progress.
Database constraints ensuring referential integrity between User, Course, and Module tables.
The signed token standard used for stateless session management and role identification.
The software layer that intercepts all responses to wrap them in a consistent envelope shape.
The Alembic-managed version control for the relational schema evolution.
A constituent part of an educational offering, characterized by a specific title and sequence order.
The Pydantic model for defining a new sequence segment within an educational offering.
The Pydantic model for returning detailed information about a specific sequence segment.
The practice of explicitly labeling specific business constraints for traceability within the logic.
The logic gate ensuring that email dispatch is excluded from the synchronous registration flow.
The object-relational mapping tool used exclusively to avoid raw SQL for all persistence operations.
An exception raised when an actor attempts to access or modify an entity they do not own.
The architectural style used for the communication interface between the client and the server.
A formalized logic constraint that must be satisfied before a specific system state transition occurs.
A long-lived token used to acquire a new short-lived access token without re-authenticating.
The operational act of delivering a Pydantic-validated response envelope to the client.
The FastAPI endpoint grouping mechanism for auth, courses, and enrolments.

items.contextual_anchor: Phase 3: Resend Email Integration as Service
items.project_definition: The provided software library for interacting with the external mail service.
The structured query language, the use of which is prohibited in favor of the object-relational mapper.
The specific version of the database toolkit used for asynchronous persistence.
The collection of Pydantic v2 models used for request validation and response shaping.
The business logic layer where complex operations and external integrations are isolated from routers.
The high-level architectural summary of the implementation plan.
The specified metric of 80%+ logic coverage to be verified in the testing phase.
The suite of pytest-asyncio cases used to validate functional requirements and role isolation.
The Pydantic model returning the access and refresh credentials to the client.
An account entity with a distinct role of either student or instructor.
The Pydantic model capturing credentials for identity verification.
The Pydantic model for creating a new account with the student role.
The Pydantic model providing safe, public-facing information about an account.
An exception raised when progress values fall outside the 0-100 integer range.
